execution_overview.overall_summary: Both planned tasks were executed in Python inside the container. I implemented bounded dependency and minor code repairs to ensure runtime package availability and fix a duplicated import typo. Task1 produced bootstrapped t-tests comparing network metrics (ASPL, CC, Q) between high- vs low-openness groups. Task2 implemented a 90% node-retention bootstrap comparing ASPL. Results show high openness had higher ASPL (opposite of greater interconnectedness), lower CC (opposite), and lower Q (support), yielding a mixed overall pattern. The 90% node-wise ASPL comparison strongly indicated higher ASPL for high openness (opposite).

deviations.description: Added runtime on-the-fly pip installation (numpy, pandas, scipy, scikit-learn, networkx, python-louvain) within the Python script to handle missing packages inside the container.
deviations.reason: Container runtime initially lacked numpy and related scientific packages.

deviations.description: Fixed a duplicated import token ('import osimport os') introduced during earlier patching and refactored import block to ensure proper fallback installation and re-import.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned semantic network analysis path (case-wise bootstrap on participant resamples; ASPL, CC, Q; Welch t-tests). Changes were limited to dependency provisioning and trivial syntax repair.

result_raw.result_text: High openness showed slightly higher ASPL than low (opposite of greater interconnectedness), lower CC (opposite), and lower Q (support), with all differences statistically significant.

conclusion.conclusion_text: Results are mixed: high openness had higher ASPL and lower CC (opposite of more interconnected networks) but lower Q (consistent with more integrated networks). Overall, no clear support for greater interconnectedness in the high openness group.

deviations.description: Added runtime on-the-fly pip installation in the Python script for missing scientific libraries.
deviations.reason: Container did not have numpy and other dependencies preinstalled.

deviations.description: Refreshed import block to robustly install and re-import dependencies if missing.
deviations.reason: Prevent potential import failures during container execution.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the 90% node-retention bootstrap ASPL comparison between high vs low openness groups exactly as planned.

result_raw.result_text: Under 90% node-wise bootstrap, high openness had higher ASPL than low (opposite direction), with a very large effect size and p ~ 0.

conclusion.conclusion_text: Task2 indicates the high openness group has longer average shortest paths than the low openness group, opposing the expectation of greater interconnectedness (lower ASPL) for high openness.
